Graded Novelty Enoding Task - 4 shapes version

Exclusions

In total, the task was run 61 times.

6 runs were not completed or were duplicates.

We excluded 3 participants due to low performance in the study phase. We calculated the confidence intervals for the probability of correct “old” responses and excluded those participants whose lower CI was below 0.33 (chance level - 25 opportunities for correct “old” response, 50 for incorrect).

We excluded 2 participants due to missed responses in test phase (<= 20).

This left 50 participants in the analysis.

The sample had a mean(±sd) age of 22.02 ± 4.821254.

The sample contained 45 women.

Comparison with GNET 1

The recognition of the prototypes compared between studies (original GNET with 5 shapes).

a = original GNET b = 4 shapes GNET

## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  a and b
## t = -3.6777, df = 124.95, p-value = 0.0003482
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.14552424 -0.04369681
## sample estimates:
## mean of x mean of y 
## 0.6597895 0.7544000
## 
## Cohen's d
## 
## d estimate: -0.5908296 (medium)
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##      lower      upper 
## -0.9429363 -0.2387228

The distribution of the data is not normal (original GNET):

## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  a
## W = 0.96925, p-value = 0.02462

The distribution of the data is not normal (current GNET):

## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  b
## W = 0.93748, p-value = 0.01073

Use a non-parametric alternative for the Welch test - Mann-Whitney-Wilcoxon U test

## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  a and b
## W = 1553.5, p-value = 0.0006097
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.15997825 -0.04000147
## sample estimates:
## difference in location 
##             -0.0800681

The overall correct categorizations in the test phase

The distribution of the test data (original GNET):

## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  a
## W = 0.99288, p-value = 0.8982

The distribution of the test data (4 shapes GNET):

## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  b
## W = 0.98034, p-value = 0.5665

The performance on the recognition test compared with a Welch test

## 
##  Welch Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  a and b
## t = -0.90224, df = 104.43, p-value = 0.369
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.8859586  0.3318534
## sample estimates:
## mean of x mean of y 
## 0.4589474 0.7360000
## 
## Cohen's d
## 
## d estimate: -0.1550763 (negligible)
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##      lower      upper 
## -0.5009085  0.1907558

Study phase

ANOVA to compare the rejection rate for each difference category; plot shows the prototype recog rate also.

Mixed models

Test phase

Models #1

## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]

## Formula: rt_resp ~ stimtype * test_quality + (1 | workerId)
